title: Graph Change Events
impact: Enables real-time sync, audit logging, cache invalidation via EventEmitter-based reactive updates

memoryOverhead.target: 0 bytes when no listeners registered (lazy init)

tasks.title: Create GraphEventEmitter Class
tasks.description: Create a type-safe EventEmitter wrapper for graph change events. Uses Node.js EventEmitter under the hood but provides type safety for event names and payloads.

implementation.purpose: Provide a type-safe event system for graph mutations. External systems can subscribe to entity/relation changes for real-time sync, audit logging, or cache invalidation.
keyDecisions: Wrap Node.js EventEmitter for broad compatibility
Type-safe event names via GraphEventType union
Support for multiple listeners per event

stepByStep.details: Create the new file in the core directory

stepByStep.action: Add imports and event type definitions
stepByStep.code:
/**
 * Graph Event Emitter
 *
 * Type-safe EventEmitter for graph change notifications.
 * Enables real-time sync, audit logging, and cache invalidation.
 *
 * @module core/GraphEventEmitter
 */

import { EventEmitter } from 'events';
import type { Entity, Relation } from '../types/types.js';

/**
 * Event types emitted by the graph.
 */
export type GraphEventType =
  | 'entityCreated'
  | 'entityUpdated'
  | 'entityDeleted'
  | 'relationCreated'
  | 'relationDeleted'
  | 'observationAdded'
  | 'observationDeleted'
  | 'graphLoaded'
  | 'graphSaved';

stepByStep.action: Add event map and listener types

acceptanceCriteria: All 9 event types defined (entityCreated, entityUpdated, entityDeleted, relationCreated, relationDeleted, observationAdded, observationDeleted, graphLoaded, graphSaved)
Each event type has corresponding payload interface
on(), once(), off(), emit() methods implemented with type safety
Class has comprehensive JSDoc with examples

tasks.title: Integrate Events into GraphStorage
tasks.description: Add lazy-initialized event emitter to GraphStorage and emit events on all mutation operations (create, update, delete for entities and relations).

implementation.purpose: Integrate the GraphEventEmitter into GraphStorage so that all mutations automatically emit events. Uses lazy initialization to avoid overhead when no listeners are registered.
keyDecisions: Lazy initialization via getter (no overhead if events not used)
Events emitted after successful mutation (not before)
EntityUpdated event captures both changes and previous values
Events emitted inside mutex lock to ensure consistency
implementation.existingCodeContext: GraphStorage.ts is ~900 lines. Add events property after existing indexes (~line 90). Emit events in saveGraph, appendEntity, appendRelation, updateEntity methods.

stepByStep.action: Add import for GraphEventEmitter at the top
stepByStep.details:
Find the imports (around line 10-15) and add:
import { GraphEventEmitter } from './GraphEventEmitter.js';

stepByStep.action: Add lazy-initialized events property after observationIndex (~line 90)
Find saveGraph method and add event emission at the end
Find loadFromDisk method and add event emission at the end
Find appendEntity method and add event emission
Find appendRelation method and add event emission
Find updateEntity method and add event emission with change tracking
Add helper method to emit entity deletion events (for use by transaction)

acceptanceCriteria: entityUpdated event emitted with changes and previousValues
Helper methods for deletion events available
No performance impact when no listeners (checks _events !== null)

tasks.title: Create Graph Events Unit Tests
tasks.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for the GraphEventEmitter class and its integration with GraphStorage.

implementation.purpose: Verify the event system works correctly for all event types, handles multiple listeners, and integrates properly with GraphStorage mutations.

stepByStep.details: Ensure the file is complete and well-formed

acceptanceCriteria: Tests for basic event emission and reception
Tests for event type payloads (entityUpdated, graphLoaded)
Test for no overhead when events unused

tasks.title: Update Barrel Exports and Documentation
tasks.description: Export the new GraphEventEmitter class and all event types from the core barrel, and update documentation.

implementation.purpose: Make the event system accessible via the public API and document its usage.
keyDecisions: Export class and all type definitions

stepByStep.details: Open the core barrel export file

stepByStep.action: Add GraphEventEmitter exports at the end of the file
stepByStep.code:
// Phase 10 Sprint 2: Graph Change Events
export { GraphEventEmitter } from './GraphEventEmitter.js';
export type {
  GraphEventType,
  GraphEventMap,
  GraphEventListener,
  GraphEventBase,
  EntityCreatedEvent,
  EntityUpdatedEvent,
  EntityDeletedEvent,
  RelationCreatedEvent,
  RelationDeletedEvent,
  ObservationAddedEvent,
  ObservationDeletedEvent,
  GraphLoadedEvent,
  GraphSavedEvent,
} from './GraphEventEmitter.js';

stepByStep.action: Add Graph Change Events documentation section

acceptanceCriteria: All event types listed in documentation

successCriteria: All four tasks completed with passing tests
GraphEventEmitter class created with type-safe API
All mutation operations emit appropriate events
No performance overhead when events unused
dependencies: Sprint 10.1 (Transaction Batching - for testing combined event emission)
notes: CRITICAL: Events must be emitted AFTER successful mutation, not before
Lazy initialization ensures zero overhead when events not used
EntityUpdated event must capture both new values AND previous values for change tracking
This sprint enables Sprint 3 (Incremental TF-IDF) to hook into events for automatic index updates
Events are emitted inside mutex lock to ensure consistency with storage state
